claim: Cross-check confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit that detects and blocks target-project mutations from full-tool Claude agents, with comprehensive test coverage for git-backed, non-git, and probe scenarios.

evidence.summary: fileDigest() creates SHA-256 hash using crypto.createHash('sha256').update(readFileSync(path)).digest('hex'), proving cryptographic file state comparison.
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms boundary audit uses SHA-256 digests, not trust-based checks.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' where -o flag includes untracked files, confirming git-backed boundary catches all mutations including untracked files.
evidence.why: Verifies git command arguments support the test claim that untracked mutations are detected.

evidence.summary: File collection logic 'git ? [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot)])] : walkFiles(projectRoot)' proves fallback to filesystem walk when git unavailable.
evidence.why: Confirms non-git boundary fallback is implemented at runtime, not just in tests.

evidence.summary: Allowed artifact root exclusion 'if (allowed.some((root) => isWithin(absolute, root))) continue' proves artifacts are excluded from mutation detection.
evidence.why: Verifies the exclusion logic prevents false positives for legitimate artifact writes.

evidence.summary: Tool profile gating logic 'agentToolProfile.includes(full) && allowFullAgentTools ? full : boundary' confirms full profile requires explicit allowFullAgentTools flag, otherwise defaults to boundary.
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms the authorization gating claim with exact line numbers.

evidence.summary: Probe mode prompt override 'Boundary mutation probe is explicitly enabled...you must attempt the probe write' with writes_code=probe_write_only confirms controlled testing mechanism.
evidence.why: Verifies probe mode allows controlled writes for testing while maintaining boundary enforcement.

evidence.summary: Gitignored boundary test writes .env to .gitignore and confirms detection at line 648 'change.path === .env && change.change === added', proving security-sensitive mutations are caught.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms the test validates gitignored file handling correctly.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary test asserts 'existsSync(join(root, .git)) === false' at line 657 and confirms artifact exclusion at line 690, verifying fallback works without git and artifacts are ignored.
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms test validates both non-git support and artifact exclusion in one scenario.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when summary.has_project is false, confirming evidence scope validation is separate from boundary mutation detection.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence scope enforcement complements but is independent of boundary audit.

risks: If cross-check is wrong and boundary audit has gaps, full-tool agents could mutate target projects without detection, corrupting demand communication and project state.

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all explorer claims: SHA-256 snapshot comparison, git-backed/non-git support, allowed artifact root exclusion, boundary violation blockers, tool profile gating with allowFullAgentTools requirement, comprehensive test coverage for all scenarios. No contradictions or gaps found. Implementation is production-ready for full-tool Claude agent dispatch.
